habits: [{"id": "h1", "name": "Smell & identify ingredients", "color": "#157A6E"}, {"id": "h2", "name": "Practice plating technique", "color": "#F15025"}, {"id": "h3", "name": "Study a recipe from memory", "color": "#54457F"}, {"id": "h4", "name": "Explore new flavor combos", "color": "#86A5D9"}, {"id": "h5", "name": "Weekly market/foraging trip", "color": "#F2AF29"}, {"id": "h6", "name": "Sketch dish concept", "color": "#5D8A9E"}]
events: [{"id": "e1", "key": "event-c5-2026-1-5", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 5, "name": "Arrival in Paris", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Separated from the clan after the flooding. Fell through a drain near the Seine. Soaked, terrified \u2014 and Paris.", "location": "Paris sewers, near \u00cele de la Cit\u00e9"}, {"id": "e2", "key": "event-c5-2026-1-6", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 6, "name": "First sneak into Gusteau's kitchen", "time": "6:30 PM", "notes": "Climbed through the ventilation. Watched the brigade at full service. Colette explaining sauce. My heart was pounding.", "location": "Gusteau's Restaurant, Paris"}, {"id": "e3", "key": "event-c5-2026-1-7", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 7, "name": "Dream of Gusteau on the rooftop", "time": "Night", "notes": "He appeared in the window reflection again. Said: 'Anyone can cook.' I believed him more than I ever had.", "location": "Paris rooftop"}, {"id": "e4", "key": "event-c2-2026-1-10", "calendarId": "c2", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 10, "name": "Night scouting of Paris alleyways", "time": "10:00 PM", "notes": "Mapped the alleyways from the sewers to Gusteau's back door. Twelve minutes if I avoided the cat near Rue de Rivoli.", "location": "Paris alleyways"}, {"id": "e5", "key": "event-c5-2026-1-12", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 12, "name": "Linguini spills the soup \u2014 I fix it", "time": "7:15 PM", "notes": "He knocked over the stock. I couldn't let it happen. Fixed it. He saw me. Everything changed.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e6", "key": "event-c1-2026-1-13", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 13, "name": "First hair-pull session with Linguini", "time": "Midnight", "notes": "Practiced the pull-and-steer method. Linguini kept flinching. We'll get there.", "location": "Linguini's apartment"}, {"id": "e7", "key": "event-c1-2026-1-14", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 14, "name": "Linguini's first shift succeeds", "time": "Lunch service", "notes": "The soup got compliments. My hands. His body. We can do this.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e8", "key": "event-c5-2026-1-16", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 16, "name": "Colette explains the kitchen", "time": "3:00 PM", "notes": "She spoke about Escoffier. I wanted to respond. Stayed hidden. Her passion for craft is real.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e9", "key": "event-c1-2026-1-20", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 20, "name": "Mapping the brigade system", "time": "All day", "notes": "Mapped every role. Colette is saucier. Horst is on fish. The hierarchy is everything here.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e10", "key": "event-c1-2026-1-22", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 22, "name": "Pre-dawn pantry ingredient audit", "time": "5:00 AM", "notes": "Crept in before the brigade arrived. Smelled everything. The tarragon was exceptional. The cream borderline sour.", "location": "Gusteau's pantry"}, {"id": "e11", "key": "event-c4-2026-1-24", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 0, "day": 24, "name": "Late-night cooking test at Linguini's", "time": "11:00 PM", "notes": "Tried a new sauce variation. Linguini kept second-guessing the salt. I wrenched left. He understood eventually.", "location": "Linguini's apartment, Paris"}, {"id": "e12", "key": "event-c5-2026-2-2", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 1, "day": 2, "name": "Rooftop night view of Paris", "time": "9:00 PM", "notes": "Climbed to the rooftop after the kitchen closed. Saw all of Paris. The lights. The smell of a hundred kitchens. This city is made of flavor.", "location": "Gusteau's rooftop"}, {"id": "e13", "key": "event-c1-2026-2-5", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 1, "day": 5, "name": "First composed dish concept", "time": "4:30 AM", "notes": "Worked out the mushroom consomm\u00e9: trim, thyme, a whisper of truffle. Asked Linguini to suggest it. Larousse nodded. We're doing it.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e14", "key": "event-c1-2026-2-6", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 1, "day": 6, "name": "Refined Linguini's soup holds", "time": "Lunch service", "notes": "The Gusteau's soup now had a secondary depth \u2014 my adjustment held. No one knows. Yet.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e15", "key": "event-c5-2026-2-10", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 1, "day": 10, "name": "Canal Saint-Martin foraging", "time": "Dawn", "notes": "Found wild sorrel along the canal bank. Also young dandelion greens and a sprig of lavender. Spring was coming early.", "location": "Canal Saint-Martin"}, {"id": "e16", "key": "event-c1-2026-2-14", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 1, "day": 14, "name": "Skinner interrogates Linguini", "time": "Afternoon", "notes": "Skinner interrogated Linguini for forty minutes. Linguini fumbled but held the story. I hid under the toque and sweated.", "location": "Gusteau's office"}, {"id": "e17", "key": "event-c1-2026-2-18", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 1, "day": 18, "name": "Skinner orders Gusteau DNA test", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Overheard through the vent. Skinner ordered a DNA test. He suspected Linguini is Gusteau's son. This changed everything.", "location": "Skinner's office"}, {"id": "e18", "key": "event-c5-2026-3-1", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 1, "name": "Discovered Gusteau's will", "time": "2:00 AM", "notes": "Snuck into Skinner's office. Found the envelope. Linguini is Gusteau's heir. Carried it out carefully.", "location": "Skinner's office"}, {"id": "e19", "key": "event-c5-2026-3-2", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 2, "name": "Delivered the will to Linguini", "time": "Early morning", "notes": "Left it on his kitchen counter. Watched from the windowsill as he opened it. His face said everything.", "location": "Linguini's apartment"}, {"id": "e20", "key": "event-c1-2026-3-5", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 5, "name": "Linguini promoted \u2014 Skinner ousted", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Skinner is out. Linguini inherits everything. The whole brigade was confused. So was he.", "location": "Gusteau's Restaurant"}, {"id": "e21", "key": "event-c4-2026-3-8", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 8, "name": "Father visits Gusteau's back door", "time": "Dusk", "notes": "He came to the back door with cousins. Warned me about the restaurant. Said it was no place for a rat. He's wrong.", "location": "Gusteau's back alley"}, {"id": "e22", "key": "event-c1-2026-3-10", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 10, "name": "Remy officially cooks in kitchen", "time": "Evening service", "notes": "Linguini told the staff I exist. Silence. Then Colette said: 'If the rat can cook, the rat cooks.' Best moment of my life.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e23", "key": "event-c4-2026-3-12", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 12, "name": "Emile visits for taste test night", "time": "Midnight", "notes": "Made him try the sorrel reduction. He just wanted cheese. I made him eat it anyway. He chewed slowly. Progress.", "location": "Linguini's apartment"}, {"id": "e24", "key": "event-c1-2026-3-15", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 15, "name": "First dish fully attributed to Remy", "time": "Dinner service", "notes": "Table 12 sent compliments to the kitchen. 'The soup tasted like someone loved it.' I kept the note.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e25", "key": "event-c1-2026-3-18", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 18, "name": "Technique standoff with Colette", "time": "Afternoon", "notes": "She showed me the standard Gusteau method. I showed her why the variation works better. Standoff. Then she tasted it.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e26", "key": "event-c5-2026-3-22", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 2, "day": 22, "name": "Rooftop reflection on three weeks", "time": "10:00 PM", "notes": "Three weeks since I first cooked here openly. I kept waiting for someone to throw me out. They didn't.", "location": "Gusteau's rooftop"}, {"id": "e27", "key": "event-c1-2026-4-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 1, "name": "Seasonal menu development session", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Linguini relayed my ideas to the brigade. Horst approved the fish pairing. Colette pushed back on the sauce \u2014 she was right.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e28", "key": "event-c1-2026-4-5", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 5, "name": "First full dinner service led by Remy", "time": "Evening service", "notes": "Controlled the whole pass. Timed every plate. Linguini kept up. The dining room was full.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e29", "key": "event-c1-2026-4-8", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 8, "name": "Sauce collaboration with Colette", "time": "Afternoon prep", "notes": "She let me guide her hand on the reduction. Didn't say anything. But she smiled at the plate.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e30", "key": "event-c2-2026-4-12", "calendarId": "c2", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 12, "name": "Market day \u2014 March\u00e9 d'Aligre", "time": "7:00 AM", "notes": "Rode in Linguini's jacket to March\u00e9 d'Aligre. The tomatoes were extraordinary. Bought half a crate.", "location": "March\u00e9 d'Aligre, Paris"}, {"id": "e31", "key": "event-c5-2026-4-15", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 15, "name": "Caught stealing food \u2014 confrontation", "time": "Late night", "notes": "Linguini caught me taking cheese. He was angry. I couldn't explain. We sat in silence. I left.", "location": "Linguini's apartment"}, {"id": "e32", "key": "event-c5-2026-4-16", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 16, "name": "Slept under Pont Neuf alone", "time": "All night", "notes": "First night without the restaurant or Linguini. Cold. Watched the Seine. Thought about the clan.", "location": "Pont Neuf, Paris"}, {"id": "e33", "key": "event-c4-2026-4-17", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 17, "name": "Reunion with the rat colony", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Dad found me by the river. Led me back. They were in a warehouse near Montmartre. It smelled of trash. Not home.", "location": "Montmartre warehouse"}, {"id": "e34", "key": "event-c1-2026-4-20", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 20, "name": "Skinner plots against Linguini", "time": "Evening", "notes": "Heard through the vent: Skinner wanted to disgrace Linguini before the Sol\u00e8ne LeClaire review. We had to stop this.", "location": "Skinner's office"}, {"id": "e35", "key": "event-c1-2026-4-22", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 3, "day": 22, "name": "Counter-sabotage overnight", "time": "2:00 AM", "notes": "Redirected the spoiled ingredients Skinner planted. Replaced with fresh stock. He wouldn't know until service.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e36", "key": "event-c5-2026-5-1", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 1, "name": "Confrontation with Linguini", "time": "Evening", "notes": "He said he didn't need me anymore. That he could cook alone. I walked out. My heart broke a little.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e37", "key": "event-c4-2026-5-2", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 2, "name": "Emile and Dad come to find Remy", "time": "Dusk", "notes": "They came to find me. Dad brought cheese. Emile talked too much. But they were there.", "location": "Paris sewer junction"}, {"id": "e38", "key": "event-c5-2026-5-5", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 5, "name": "Ego announces his review", "time": "Morning", "notes": "The whole brigade read the card at briefing. 'I will be reviewing Gusteau's.' Silence. Then chaos.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e39", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-6", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 6, "name": "Emergency pre-review prep begins", "time": "All day", "notes": "Twelve-hour prep session. I designed three possible menus. Linguini memorized the key movements.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e40", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-8", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 8, "name": "Research session on Anton Ego", "time": "Midnight", "notes": "Found old newspaper clippings at the library. Ego's writing style: theatrical, precise, cruel. He looks for truth.", "location": "Paris library, Rue de Richelieu"}, {"id": "e41", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-10", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 10, "name": "Review menu finalized", "time": "Afternoon", "notes": "Settled on a ratatouille as the centerpiece. Simple. Humble. Ancestral. The opposite of what he expected.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e42", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-12", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 12, "name": "Staff briefing before Ego's review", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Linguini addressed everyone. Said tonight would define us. I watched from the vent. I already knew what I had to do.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e43", "key": "event-c4-2026-5-14", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 14, "name": "Rat clan arrives to work the kitchen", "time": "5:00 PM", "notes": "Dad led the clan in through the back. Emile organized the supply line. Cousins at every station. Chaos \u2014 beautiful chaos.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e44", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-14", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 14, "name": "Remy takes the chef's station", "time": "6:00 PM", "notes": "Linguini told the brigade the truth. Some left. The rats stayed. I climbed to the chef's station. This was it.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e45", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-14", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 14, "name": "Ratatouille prepared for Ego", "time": "7:45 PM", "notes": "Confit byaldi. Every slice paper-thin. Every layer exact. I cooked it for the memory of Gusteau. And for myself.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e46", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-14", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 14, "name": "Ego tastes the ratatouille", "time": "8:30 PM", "notes": "He took one bite. The pen stopped. He sat very still. I watched through the porthole. He was gone somewhere else.", "location": "Gusteau's dining room"}, {"id": "e47", "key": "event-c1-2026-5-14", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 14, "name": "Ego requests to meet the chef", "time": "10:30 PM", "notes": "He waited in the dining room after everyone left. Linguini walked him back. The door opened. Ego saw me.", "location": "Gusteau's kitchen"}, {"id": "e48", "key": "event-c5-2026-5-16", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 16, "name": "Ego's five-star review published", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Read it on a newspaper someone left by a bench. 'The finest meal I have had in years. The chef is nothing less than the best.'", "location": "Paris newsstand"}, {"id": "e49", "key": "event-c5-2026-5-17", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 17, "name": "Health inspectors raid Gusteau's", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Someone tipped them off. The rats were discovered. Gusteau's closed. Linguini lost the restaurant.", "location": "Gusteau's Restaurant"}, {"id": "e50", "key": "event-c5-2026-5-18", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 18, "name": "Ego loses his position", "time": "All day", "notes": "He had vouched for the chef. Lost his job, his prestige. I heard he smiled anyway.", "location": "Paris"}, {"id": "e51", "key": "event-c4-2026-5-20", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 20, "name": "Father gives Remy his blessing", "time": "Dusk", "notes": "He came to the river. This time he brought nothing. Just said: 'You cook. That's what you are.' I had been waiting for those words.", "location": "Seine riverbank"}, {"id": "e52", "key": "event-c5-2026-5-22", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 4, "day": 22, "name": "Final walk through Gusteau's", "time": "Night", "notes": "One last time through the kitchen. The hearth was cold. I memorized every surface.", "location": "Gusteau's Restaurant, closed"}, {"id": "e53", "key": "event-c1-2026-6-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 5, "day": 1, "name": "La Petite Cuisine opens", "time": "All day", "notes": "Linguini found the space. Small, perfect. Colette came back. We built the menu together.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, Paris"}, {"id": "e54", "key": "event-c1-2026-6-2", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 5, "day": 2, "name": "First dinner service at new restaurant", "time": "Evening", "notes": "Twelve covers. Every single plate perfect. Emile ate at table three. He cried. I ignored this.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, Paris"}, {"id": "e55", "key": "event-c1-2026-6-5", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 5, "day": 5, "name": "Ego becomes a regular", "time": "Lunch", "notes": "He walked in at noon with a reservation under 'A. Ego.' Ordered the ratatouille. Ate slowly. Tipped generously.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, Paris"}, {"id": "e56", "key": "event-c1-2026-6-10", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 5, "day": 10, "name": "Spring menu revision with Colette", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Three new dishes. Her knife work is extraordinary. She said I have 'the instincts of a mother cook.' I think this is a compliment.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e57", "key": "event-c4-2026-6-12", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 5, "day": 12, "name": "Rat clan prep session at new kitchen", "time": "Dawn", "notes": "Dad brought the whole clan for pre-service prep. Emile was a reliable peeler. Cousins were surprisingly disciplined.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e58", "key": "event-c4-2026-6-17", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 5, "day": 17, "name": "Emile's first weekly Saturday visit", "time": "Evening", "notes": "He came every Saturday. I always made something outside the menu. Last week: a fig and goat cheese bruschetta. He wept again.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine"}, {"id": "e59", "key": "event-c1-2026-7-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 6, "day": 1, "name": "Summer menu planning session", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Tomatoes, zucchini blossoms, fresh basil. The market was exploding. The menu wrote itself in June.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e60", "key": "event-c2-2026-7-5", "calendarId": "c2", "year": 2026, "month": 6, "day": 5, "name": "Sourcing trip to Provence", "time": "All day", "notes": "Rode with Linguini. The lavender fields. The olive groves. The air. I brought back enough herbs for a month.", "location": "Provence"}, {"id": "e61", "key": "event-c3-2026-7-14", "calendarId": "c3", "year": 2026, "month": 6, "day": 14, "name": "Bastille Day special menu", "time": "All day", "notes": "Six-course all-French-classics menu. Sold out two weeks in advance. Colette and I worked twelve hours.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine"}, {"id": "e62", "key": "event-c4-2026-7-15", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 6, "day": 15, "name": "Father's birthday feast", "time": "Evening", "notes": "Made his favorite: a hearty grain-and-root stew. He pretended he wasn't moved. He was.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, private dining"}, {"id": "e63", "key": "event-c1-2026-8-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 7, "day": 1, "name": "100th dinner service milestone", "time": "Evening", "notes": "We toasted with a single glass of wine each. Ego was there. He raised his glass to me.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine"}, {"id": "e64", "key": "event-c5-2026-8-10", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 7, "day": 10, "name": "Started personal recipe notebook", "time": "Midnight", "notes": "Linguini found me a small journal. I pressed herbs between the pages. Colette laughed when she found thyme in the risotto notes.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e65", "key": "event-c4-2026-8-15", "calendarId": "c4", "year": 2026, "month": 7, "day": 15, "name": "Emile joins the early prep team", "time": "Morning", "notes": "He's officially on the early-morning vegetable prep. Slow, careful, surprisingly good at supreming citrus.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e66", "key": "event-c1-2026-9-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 8, "day": 1, "name": "Autumn harvest menu launch", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Butternut squash, pears, walnuts, aged comt\u00e9. The season changed everything. The kitchen smelled different.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e67", "key": "event-c2-2026-9-15", "calendarId": "c2", "year": 2026, "month": 8, "day": 15, "name": "Normandy dairy sourcing visit", "time": "All day", "notes": "Sourced a new butter. Single-herd. 84% fat. Changed my croissant recipe immediately.", "location": "Normandy"}, {"id": "e68", "key": "event-c1-2026-10-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 9, "day": 1, "name": "Suspected Michelin inspector visit", "time": "Evening service", "notes": "A quiet man at table six. Ordered methodically. Tasted without expression. I cooked every element myself.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine"}, {"id": "e69", "key": "event-c1-2026-11-1", "calendarId": "c1", "year": 2026, "month": 10, "day": 1, "name": "Winter menu planning session", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Braised short rib. Potato gratin with black truffle. A simple chocolate fondant. The cold called for weight.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e70", "key": "event-c5-2026-12-1", "calendarId": "c5", "year": 2026, "month": 11, "day": 1, "name": "One year anniversary in Paris", "time": "Night", "notes": "Sat on the rooftop. The city was wet. November Paris smells of woodsmoke and chestnuts. Patterns tell you what you're becoming.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}, {"id": "e86", "key": "event-c6-2026-12-1", "calendarId": "c6", "year": 2026, "month": 11, "day": 1, "name": "Plan next year menu", "time": "Morning", "notes": "Started dreaming the January menu before December ended.", "location": "La Petite Cuisine, kitchen"}]
journal-j1-2026-1-6:
**Lesson:** A professional kitchen smells nothing like the countryside.

Butter, wine, stock — and underneath all of it, something I could only call *intention*. I pressed my nose to the grate for a full hour tonight.

I had been reading cookbooks my whole life. That was the first time I understood the difference between reading and *knowing*.
journal-j1-2026-1-13:
**Lesson:** Trust requires demonstration, not explanation.

Linguini could have screamed. He didn't. I could have run. I didn't. We looked at each other across the kitchen floor for what felt like a very long time. Then I reached for his hair.

That was the beginning.
journal-j1-2026-1-16:
**Lesson:** Passion in a kitchen is contagious.

Colette spoke about Escoffier as if he were still alive in the sauces. I wanted to tap the glass and say — *yes, exactly that*. A cook who reveres the craft: that's someone worth learning from.
journal-j1-2026-1-22:
**Lesson:** The toque is not about hiding. It's about operating.

Almost got caught three times today. My grip on the left side of his hair was too loose — he listed into the pass. Had to compensate.

Adjustment: lean pressure, not pull pressure.
journal-j1-2026-2-5:
**Lesson:** Skinner is not stupid. He is suspicious. These are different problems.

A stupid adversary makes mistakes. A suspicious one watches everything. I had to be more careful in the vents. He paused under duct 7 for no reason today.
journal-j1-2026-2-14:
**Lesson:** Even in a kitchen run by fear, good food breaks through.

A table of eight sent back compliments on the consommé. Skinner was furious the attention went to Linguini. The soup didn't care. It was just good.
journal-j1-2026-2-22:
**Lesson:** You cannot taste with your eyes.

Practiced identifying twelve ingredients blind tonight. Got eleven. The twelfth — star anise hidden in a fennel base — fooled me. The lesson: star anise doesn't announce itself. It whispers.
journal-j1-2026-3-5:
**Lesson:** The brigade system is a kind of music.

Everyone in their place, everyone on tempo. When Linguini missed a cue tonight, the whole line felt it. I had to compensate at the pass. A kitchen is not a solo — it is an orchestra. I had to remember I was the conductor, not the only instrument.
journal-j1-2026-3-15:
**Lesson:** You cannot hide who you are forever.

Linguini tried to explain the soup adjustment without mentioning me. He stumbled. Colette noticed. I could feel her suspicion through the toque. The truth has a way of surfacing, like cream.
journal-j1-2026-3-22:
**Lesson:** Sabotage has a smell.

Skinner's planted ingredients — the off-cream, the slightly aged fish — each had a specific wrong note. I catalogued them. A cook must know when the kitchen has been tampered with. This is a survival skill, not just craft.
journal-j1-2026-4-1:
**Lesson:** Separation is sometimes clarifying.

A night alone by the Seine. Cold stone. No kitchen smells. Just water and wind.

I realized: I didn't cook because of Linguini's hands. I cooked because I was made to cook. The partnership mattered. But the vocation was mine alone.
journal-j1-2026-4-6:
**Lesson:** Preparation is the only antidote to fear.

Ego's review was coming. The brigade was frightened. I was not frightened. I was focused. There is a difference.

Fear paralyzes. Preparation moves.
journal-j1-2026-4-14:
**Lesson:** Simplicity is the hardest achievement.

I could have made something complex tonight. Technically impressive. Multi-component. Instead I made ratatouille. A peasant dish. A grandmother's dish.

Ego stopped chewing. His pen fell.

Simplicity done perfectly is the highest form.
journal-j1-2026-4-16:
**Lesson:** A great critic's greatest act is recognizing greatness they didn't expect.

Ego changed his mind publicly. Completely. At personal cost. That took a kind of courage most people never need.

Respect.
journal-j1-2026-4-20:
**Lesson:** A father's approval arrives in its own time.

Dad stood by the river and said 'you cook.' Three words. It was enough. It was everything.

I had been cooking my whole life without it. Now I had it. The food didn't taste different. But I did.
journal-j1-2026-5-1:
**Lesson:** A small kitchen with purpose beats a grand one with fear.

La Petite Cuisine holds twenty-four covers. Gusteau's held ninety. The food at my small table tonight was better than anything I made when I was terrified of Skinner's footsteps.

Fear shrinks. Freedom expands.
journal-j1-2026-5-10:
**Lesson:** Collaboration requires surrender on both sides.

Colette pushed back on my beurre blanc temperature today. I was wrong and she was right. I showed her, not with words, but by tasting both versions side by side.

She nodded once. That was our entire argument and resolution.
journal-j1-2026-6-5:
**Lesson:** Sourcing is cooking.

The lavender from Provence this morning changed three recipes before I got back to Paris. You cannot separate the ingredient from its origin. A tomato grown in mediocre soil tells you in the sauce.
journal-j1-2026-7-10:
**Lesson:** Document what you know while you know it.

Started the recipe notebook tonight. Not just recipes — techniques, failures, discoveries. The moment in January when I fixed Linguini's soup? I almost forgot the exact adjustment. Almost.

A cook with no record is a cook with no history.
journal-j1-2026-8-15:
**Lesson:** The source changes the ingredient.

Normandy butter. 84% fat. Single herd. The flavor was not louder — it was *deeper*. I didn't taste butter more. I tasted it *more truthfully*.

Lesson: ingredient quality is not about volume. It is about fidelity.
journal-j1-2026-9-1:
**Lesson:** A Michelin inspector eats methodically and says nothing.

The man at table six ordered in sequence, tasted between each, and left no visible reaction. I cooked every element of his meal personally.

Whether he was an inspector or simply a thorough diner: both deserved the same dish.
journal-j1-2026-10-1:
**Lesson:** Seasons teach humility.

Summer's confidence — the tomatoes, the basil, the easy brightness — was gone. October asked harder questions. What do you do with root vegetables and long braises? You plan. You wait. You trust the cold.
journal-j1-2026-11-1:
**Lesson:** A year is both short and complete.

I arrived in January. A lost rat in a sewer. I closed November on a rooftop with a kitchen I built, a brigade I trusted, and a critic who came every week.

Anyone can cook. I knew this now from the inside.
journal-j1-2026-11-31:
**Lesson:** The best service is the one that ends with more to do.

New Year's Eve. Twelve courses. Every table full. Ego's toast at midnight.

I was already thinking about February's menu. That is how you know you're exactly where you should be.
journal-j2-2026-1-5:
1. Fresh start. Paris at dawn.

2. That I had a nose capable of this city.

3. Gusteau's voice: *you belong here.*
journal-j2-2026-1-13:
1. Linguini didn't scream.

2. The kitchen after everyone left. The stillness.

3. The smell of proper stock reducing on a low flame.
journal-j2-2026-1-16:
1. Colette's hands. She moved through a kitchen like water through stone — inevitable.

2. That I could taste the difference between today's herbs and yesterday's.

3. The first proper consommé I made in a professional kitchen. Clear as glass.
journal-j2-2026-2-2:
1. The rooftop. Paris from above.

2. Emile tried the sorrel reduction. He hated it. But he tried it. That counted.

3. The fact that I was here at all.
journal-j2-2026-2-10:
1. A perfect piece of cheese — aged comté, thirty-six months. Found near the back of the pantry.

2. Linguini was improving. He only flinched twice during the evening service.

3. The way a kitchen smells at the end of a good service: exhausted and satisfied.
journal-j2-2026-2-24:
1. Wild sorrel from the canal. The bitterness was perfect.

2. That the city ignored me mostly. I was free in it.

3. Gusteau's words in the window reflection tonight. Still there.
journal-j2-2026-3-5:
1. The brigade accepted a dish suggestion today without knowing it came from me.

2. Horst plated the fish course with genuine care.

3. One plate came back with a note: 'best meal this year.' I kept the note.
journal-j2-2026-3-12:
1. Marché d'Aligre at 7am. The vendors, the noise, the tomatoes still warm from Provence.

2. That I could identify a good tomato by smell from three meters.

3. Linguini let me choose every purchase today. He trusted my nose completely.
journal-j2-2026-3-22:
1. The city after rain. Petrichor mixed with bread from a boulangerie on Rue Saint-Jacques.

2. That I had a home, even if it was a borrowed corner of a Paris kitchen.

3. Colette smiled at the sauce today. She didn't say anything. She smiled.
journal-j2-2026-4-6:
1. The kitchen in the morning before service — the cold stoves, the ordered mise en place.

2. Dad's voice warning me. He worried. That meant something.

3. I had a plan for Ego's night. I was grateful for clarity.
journal-j2-2026-4-14:
1. The clan. Every one of them. They came.

2. The sound of twenty rats working in coordinated silence.

3. The moment Ego's pen stopped. I will be grateful for that for the rest of my life.
journal-j2-2026-4-20:
1. The river at dusk. Dad beside it.

2. That he came. That he understood.

3. The weight of his approval, arriving at exactly the right time.
journal-j2-2026-5-1:
1. La Petite Cuisine. The name. The size. The rightness.

2. Colette, who came back.

3. The first service where every plate went out the way I had imagined it.
journal-j2-2026-5-5:
1. Ego walked in with a reservation.

2. He ordered the ratatouille.

3. He ate slowly, thoughtfully, and left a very large tip.
journal-j2-2026-5-17:
1. Emile. My ridiculous, generous, emotional brother.

2. He showed up every Saturday. Table four. Always.

3. His face every single time a dish was better than he expected.
journal-j2-2026-6-5:
1. Lavender. The real thing, not distilled.

2. Provence. Even for one day.

3. That I had work that took me toward beauty.
journal-j2-2026-6-14:
1. Paris celebrating itself.

2. A sold-out Bastille Day menu.

3. The moment at the pass when every dish on the six-course menu was perfect, simultaneously.
journal-j2-2026-6-15:
1. Dad. His birthday. His face.

2. That he ate the grain stew and said nothing — because his silence meant it was good.

3. The table where he sat with Emile, quietly, like a proper family.
journal-j2-2026-7-1:
1. One hundred services. One hundred chances to get it right.

2. Ego's raised glass.

3. The kitchen still standing.
journal-j2-2026-8-15:
1. Normandy butter. Worth the trip entirely.

2. That I could build a supply chain built on trust and quality.

3. The way Colette reacted when she tasted the new croissant: quiet, wide-eyed, a small nod.
journal-j2-2026-10-15:
1. Autumn Paris. The leaves in the Tuileries.

2. A truffle delivery that arrived exactly on time.

3. Potato gratin, made perfectly, feeding a full dining room.
journal-j2-2026-11-1:
1. November rooftop. The whole city stretched out.

2. One year in Paris. I made it.

3. The kitchen waiting for me tomorrow.
journal-j2-2026-11-24:
1. Every table full on Christmas Eve.

2. The child who called me a rat. She wasn't wrong. But she saw me.

3. The sound of a restaurant at capacity — plates, voices, laughter. The fullest sound in the world.
journal-j2-2026-11-31:
1. Twelve courses served without error.

2. Ego at table one.

3. Tomorrow. The kitchen. The next year. Everything still ahead.
journal-j3-2026-1-5: Note to self: the ventilation shaft between the larder and the fish station has a loose grate. Third screw from the left is stripped. Do not lean on it during service.
journal-j3-2026-1-10:
Linguini made an omelette tonight without my help. It was technically correct and completely flavorless. He needed to learn that cooking without emotion produces food without soul.

Also: he used margarine instead of butter. We had words. (I pulled left sharply. He understood.)
journal-j3-2026-1-18:
Colette carried her knife roll in a gray canvas wrap with a burgundy tie. Custom made, I thought. She set it down the same way every morning — left to right, perfectly parallel to the board.

This kind of ritual is not superstition. It is respect.
journal-j3-2026-2-8:
Emile came by the apartment. I made him taste five things blind. He got one right. Then he found a piece of Brie I'd been saving and ate the entire thing in forty seconds.

I love him. He is a disaster.
journal-j3-2026-2-18:
The mushroom consommé was nearly right. The issue was the final reduction — I was losing clarity chasing depth. They are not opposites. Thought harder.

Option A: reduce at lower heat, longer time.
Option B: clarify with egg white raft after reduction.
Option B. Obviously Option B.
journal-j3-2026-2-28:
Skinner walked past duct 7 again at 11:40pm. Third night in a row. He suspected something. He just didn't know what.

I moved to duct 4 as my primary observation point. Better angle on the garde manger.
journal-j3-2026-3-8:
Colette and I had what I would describe as our first creative disagreement today. She wanted the sauce lighter. I wanted depth. Neither of us could speak to the other directly.

Linguini translated through indirect suggestions. Eventually she tasted my version. Paused. Said 'okay.' That was a victory.
journal-j3-2026-3-15:
Skinner was gone. The document was real. Linguini owned the restaurant now.

I felt something I didn't have a word for. Something between relief and vertigo. The kitchen was still the kitchen. But the kitchen was different.
journal-j3-2026-3-20:
Planning note: before Ego's review, I needed to:
- Audit all current ingredient stocks
- Test the ratatouille at least four times
- Identify which brigade members would stay under pressure
- Prepare a backup dish

The backup dish: a simple roast chicken with herbs. If everything else failed, perfect roast chicken tells the truth.
journal-j3-2026-4-2:
By the river with Emile and Dad.

Emile talked the entire time. Dad said very little. I sat between them and thought about how strange it was that the people who understood me least were sometimes the ones I most wanted to have near me.
journal-j3-2026-4-8:
Research notes on Anton Ego:
- Former top critic at Le Monde Gastronomique
- Gave Gusteau's original two-star review after the incident
- His writing style: scalpel, not hammer
- He didn't hate food. He expected it to be worthy of him.

The ratatouille was worthy.
journal-j3-2026-4-14:
Tonight I cooked the most important meal of my life.

I was afraid for exactly thirty seconds, standing at the chef's station with the whole kitchen quiet. Then the heat came on and the smell hit me and there was nothing left in my head except the dish.

The ratatouille. Every slice. Every layer.

Ego stopped chewing.
journal-j3-2026-4-22:
One last walk through Gusteau's. The cold hearth. The dark pass. The still stockpots.

I memorized the geometry of it — the way the light came through the porthole, the exact height of the counter edge, the sound your claws make on the tile near the dry storage.

Goodbye, Gusteau's. You were the beginning.
journal-j3-2026-5-2:
La Petite Cuisine note: the oven on the left ran 10°C hot. Adjusted accordingly. The extraction fan in the corner was louder than expected — worth monitoring but not yet a concern.

The back counter was the perfect height. First time in my life a counter had been the right height.
journal-j3-2026-5-12:
Emile had been designated head of vegetable prep on early shifts. He was surprisingly precise with a mandoline. Slow, but precise.

Dad supervised the cousins. He organized them in a way I recognized. It was the same system he used to manage the clan. Turns out foraging and mise en place were the same skill.
journal-j3-2026-6-1:
June menu notes:
- Zucchini blossom fritters: batter had to stay cold. Non-negotiable.
- The basil oil: blended at the last possible moment before service.
- Strawberry sorbet: no added sugar. The Gariguette variety from the market was sweet enough.

Let the season do the work. June required almost nothing from the cook except restraint.
journal-j3-2026-7-10:
Recipe notebook, entry 1:
Mushroom consommé — clarified with egg white raft. Final ratio: 200g mushroom trim per liter water. Time: 40 minutes gentle simmer, 15 minutes rest.

This dish saved me. Or started something that saved me. It stays on the menu forever.
journal-j3-2026-8-2:
August observation: the dining room had a particular quality of light at 8:15pm in summer. Gold, horizontal, falling across table six in a way that made every dish look like it belonged in a painting.

Service at 8:15pm in August was the best service of the year.
journal-j3-2026-9-15:
Post-Michelin-visit analysis (if he was a Michelin inspector):
- He chose: amuse bouche, two courses, cheese, dessert
- No wine
- Wrote nothing visible
- Spent exactly 97 minutes

I cooked everything for table six that night personally. Whatever he found: it was the real thing.
journal-j3-2026-10-10:
Winter prep note: the truffle delivery schedule had to shift to Thursdays to hit the Friday service properly. Current Monday delivery meant two-day age on a product that wanted freshness.

Also: the gratin needed 15 minutes more than the recipe said. Every time. Trust the crust.
journal-j3-2026-11-10:
November: thinking about next year's spring menu already.

The asparagus season was six weeks if we were lucky. The menu had to be ready before the first asparagus arrived. A chef who waited for the ingredient to inspire the dish wasted the first two weeks.

Prepare for the season before the season arrives.
journal-j3-2026-11-28:
End of year reflection:

I arrived in Paris in January as a rat who had read too many cookbooks and dreamed too large.

I finished the year as a rat who had cooked one hundred and forty-seven services, fed a critic who once destroyed my hero, earned a father's respect, and built something real.

Gusteau said: anyone can cook. I think he meant: anyone who was willing to risk being exactly who they are.
